title: Encyclia flava
taxonomy: Encyclia flava is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Encyclia, which belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the monocot group of flowering plants, characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. The family Orchidaceae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, comprising over 30,000 species worldwide. Encyclia flava is further categorized within the subtribe Encycliinae, a group of orchids known for their showy, funnel-shaped flowers. The specific classification of Encyclia flava reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the orchid family.

benefits: Encyclia flava, a type of orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it potentially useful in wound healing and preventing infections. The plant also contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. intro: Encyclia flava, also known as the Yellow Encyclia or Yellow Orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species native to the Americas, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Encyclia flava include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Encyclia flava is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine practices in Central and South America.
